project_overview.project_name: Adolescent Girls Initiative for Learning and Empowerment (AGILE) Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve secondary education opportunities among girls in targeted areas in participating states
project_overview.beneficiaries: 6.02 million girls and boys in secondary schools; 15.5 million family and community members; 13,400+ teachers and administrative staff

geographic_scope.coverage_type: Multi-state (7 participating states covering 182 Local Government Authorities)
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Yes - Borno state is conflict-affected by Boko Haram insurgency; North East, Middle Belt, and Niger Delta regions experience widespread conflict; project includes specific adaptations for fragile and conflict-affected contexts

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Creating Safe and Accessible Learning Spaces
key_components.description: Construction of 440 new JSSs and 220 new SSSs; rehabilitation of existing infrastructure in 2,786 JSSs and 1,914 SSSs; provision of teaching and learning materials; WASH facilities; climate-smart infrastructure

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Fostering an Enabling Environment for Girls
key_components.description: Social and behavioral change campaigns; life skills training through safe spaces; digital literacy and remote learning platform; girls' scholarship program (financial incentives to poorest households)

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Project Management and System Strengthening
key_components.description: System strengthening for secondary EMIS; technical assistance; capacity building; project management; monitoring and evaluation; support for policy development
Funds to be allocated during mid-term review based on state performance

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Education (FME) at federal level; State Ministries of Education (SMEs) at state level; with support from National Project Coordination Unit (NPCU) and State Project Implementation Units (SPIUs)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National Project Steering Committee (NPSC) chaired by Minister of Education; State Project Steering Committees (SPSCs) chaired by State Commissioners of Education; multi-sectoral coordination with Ministries of Women Affairs, Youth, Health, and Humanitarian Affairs
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Community-driven development (CDD) for JSS construction; SME-led approach for SSS construction; Request for Bids/Quotations; approximately 90% of procurements at state level; World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Multi-layered M&E system grounded in country systems; third-party monitoring including ICT and geo-enabling technology; semi-annual implementation support missions; annual joint reviews; mid-term and end-term reviews; Results Framework with PDO and intermediate indicators

identified_risks.risk_description: Nigeria's economy heavily dependent on oil revenues; COVID-19 pandemic causing economic contraction (projected -3% in 2020); significant fiscal deficit; reduced FAAC allocations to states; states' inability to recruit and pay teachers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Securing governors' ascent on teacher recruitment as disbursement condition; project design emphasizes sustainability; government commitment to include teacher salaries in state budgets; flexible implementation approach

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited capacity of implementing institutions; coordination difficulties between entities; capacity limitations in financial management and procurement at community and school levels; weak enforcement of environmental and social requirements
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Simplified project design; extensive capacity-building support; technical assistance at federal and state levels; emphasis on skills transfer; training on World Bank procedures; hiring of experienced consultants; community training on construction management and E&S compliance

identified_risks.risk_description: Lack of experienced procurement staff; inadequate contract management skills; limited FM capacity at community and school levels; SBMCs and CPMCs have low capacity in FM; potential for misuse of funds; unretired advances
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FM training before project effectiveness; deployment of computerized accounting systems; regular FM reviews; internal and external audits; enhanced accountability framework for training and workshops; social accountability mechanisms; involvement of SBMCs in school governance; third-party monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: School-based violence including GBV/SEA; labor influx during construction increasing SEA risks; potential displacement from land acquisition; exclusion of persons with disabilities; intercommunity conflicts over social norms promotion; elite capture in SIG management; community disputes over beneficiary targeting; limited supervision capacity in conflict-affected areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Whole school approach to violence prevention; development of Codes of Conduct with enforcement mechanisms; GBV/SEA assessment and costed action plan; mapping of GBV service providers; establishment of referral pathways; grievance redress mechanisms with GBV protocols; Security Risk Assessment for Borno; third-party monitoring in hard-to-reach areas; inclusive design following CPTED principles; extensive stakeholder engagement; community-led targeting processes

identified_risks.risk_description: Political interference in procurement; potential for corruption; weak enforcement of laws; government commitment may wane
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continuous engagement with political class; involvement of anti-corruption agencies; strong federal and state government commitment demonstrated through legislation and policy; community monitoring mechanisms; transparency in procurement and beneficiary selection

identified_risks.risk_description: Boko Haram insurgency in North East; farmer-herder conflicts; ongoing violence in Middle Belt and Niger Delta; limited capacity for supervision in conflict-affected areas; risks to beneficiaries and project staff
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security Risk Assessment to be conducted within 6 months of effectiveness; Security Management Plan as part of site-specific ESMPs; third-party monitoring in hard-to-supervise areas; use of security personnel following ESS4 requirements; adaptation of implementation measures for conflict-affected settings; linkages with Multi-Sectoral Crisis Recovery Project for North Eastern Nigeria

identified_risks.risk_description: School closures disrupting education; girls forced out of school may not return; learning gaps; economic hardship increasing vulnerability; gender divide exacerbation; weakened education systems from budget cuts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Remote learning platform with offline and online access; TV and radio broadcasting of lessons; digital literacy training; psychosocial support for girls; flexible implementation allowing for school readiness support; support for school re-opening strategies; TA on COVID-19 response policies

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS2: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Multi-level project GRM at community, SPIU, and NPCU levels; labor-specific GRM for project workers; GBV-specific protocols ensuring confidentiality and survivor-centered approach; referral pathways to GBV service providers; accessible complaint mechanisms including complaint boxes; escalation procedures; court redress as final resort
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project specifically targets adolescent girls ages 10-20; addresses gender-based violence and sexual exploitation; promotes girls' education and empowerment; includes life skills training on gender awareness; supports female teacher recruitment (minimum 50% of new teachers); female counselors lead safe space sessions; scholarships target girls; whole school approach includes boys' engagement on gender issues and toxic masculinity
vulnerable_groups: Girls in northern Nigeria (high early marriage and fertility rates)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project design explicitly accounts for fragility and conflict context; adapted implementation measures for conflict-affected states (particularly Borno); Security Risk Assessment required; Security Management Plan as part of ESMPs; third-party monitoring in hard-to-reach areas; linkages with Multi-Sectoral Crisis Recovery Project for North Eastern Nigeria; conflict-sensitive communications campaigns; psychosocial support training for teachers; focus on building resilience and social cohesion through education
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Nigeria faces security challenges exacerbating fragility in marginalized areas, particularly northern regions; Boko Haram insurgency in North East has deleterious effects on economic and social wellbeing; widespread conflict in North East, Middle Belt, and Niger Delta; complex interrelated historical, economic, political, socio-cultural and environmental factors driving conflict; project targets states with low human development outcomes, high poverty, and conflict-related challenges; North-South divide in development outcomes
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Boko Haram targeting of schools creating reluctance to send girls to school; long distances to schools placing girls at risk of sexual exploitation and abuse; corporal punishment and physical disciplinary action commonplace; limited understanding of GBV among communities; risks of violence in and around schools; labor influx during construction increasing SEA risks; security personnel to be used following ESS4 requirements; safe pathways to schools following CPTED principles; perimeter fencing and well-lit facilities; community mapping of safe/unsafe zones
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Internally displaced persons (IDPs) identified as vulnerable group; project aims to support inclusion of IDPs; Borno state affected by Boko Haram displacement; project includes support for psychosocial needs of girls in conflict-affected areas; linkages with humanitarian response programs; study planned to assess barriers for girls with disabilities and other vulnerable groups including IDPs
